Home / Services / Water Heater Repair & Installation

Water Heater Repair & Installation

Reliable repairs and efficient installations for all types of water heaters.

A malfunctioning water heater can disrupt your daily routine, especially during San Jose's cooler months. Plumbing & Drainage Pros San Jose specializes in the repair and installation of all types of water heaters, including traditional tank water heaters, tankless water heaters, and heat pump water heaters.

Our experienced technicians can diagnose and repair a wide range of water heater problems, from leaks and strange noises to inconsistent water temperatures. We use only high-quality replacement parts to ensure lasting repairs. If your water heater is beyond repair, we can help you choose a new, energy-efficient model that meets your needs and budget.

We handle the entire installation process, from removing your old water heater to connecting the new one and ensuring it's functioning properly. We'll also explain how to operate and maintain your new water heater to maximize its lifespan. With San Jose's varied housing stock, we've seen it all and can handle any water heater challenge.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Diagnosis of the water heater problem.
  2. 02 Step 2: Repair or replacement of faulty parts.
  3. 03 Step 3: Installation of new water heaters, including proper venting and connections.
  4. 04 Step 4: Testing and ensuring proper operation of the water heater.

Benefits

  • Restores hot water quickly
  • Improves energy efficiency
  • Extends the lifespan of your water heater
  • Ensures safe and reliable operation

Frequently Asked Questions

How long does a water heater typically last?

The lifespan of a water heater depends on the type and quality, but most last between 8 and 12 years. Regular maintenance can help extend its lifespan.

What are the benefits of a tankless water heater?

Tankless water heaters provide on-demand hot water, eliminate standby heat loss, and can save you money on your energy bills. They also take up less space than traditional tank water heaters.

How do I know if my water heater needs to be replaced?

Signs that your water heater may need to be replaced include frequent leaks, rusty water, strange noises, and inconsistent water temperatures.
